title: Authorities allege man threatened officers with knife before fatal shooting in Westminster
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A 22-year-old man died after being shot by Westminster police during a confrontation at his home on South Ashburnham Road.
The man allegedly threatened officers with a knife when they entered his residence to serve an arrest warrant around 4:40 p.m.
The officers involved are on administrative leave while State Police and Westminster police investigate the incident.

title: Rachel Dratch has a hairdresser to thank for viral ‘Celebrity Jeopardy!’ moment
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Rachel Dratch's reaction to a 'Celebrity Jeopardy! All Stars' clue went viral, earning millions of views on social media.
During 'Late Night with Seth Meyers,' Dratch revealed her hairdresser, Eddie Cook, inadvertently helped her with a Jeopardy! clue.
Dratch lost to Katie Nolan in the 2024 'Celebrity Jeopardy!' tournament but had a memorable viral moment on the show.

title: She survived a brain aneurysm — now she’s finishing the Marathon Majors in Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Andrea Calleja, from Manila, survived a ruptured brain aneurysm and will run the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Calleja completed marathons in New York, Berlin, Chicago, Tokyo, London, and Sydney before Boston after her recovery.
She volunteers with the Brain Aneurysm Foundation, using her story to advocate for those with similar challenges.

title: Pop-up bar dedicated to women’s sports now open in Boston
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: 261 at Para Maria, a pop-up bar at The Envoy Hotel, celebrates women's sports and is open until April 30.
The bar is named after Kathrine Switzer's Boston Marathon bib number and offers themed food and drinks.
A portion of sales supports Dream Big!, aiding girls and young women with sports equipment and training.

title: Monica Cannon-Grant ordered to pay more than $224K over fraud charges
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Monica Cannon-Grant ordered to pay over $224,000 for fraud involving pandemic relief and diverted donations.
She pleaded guilty to fraud charges and received six months home confinement and four years probation.
Federal Judge Angel Kelley set forfeiture at $224,063, including $181,000 in personal use donations and fraudulent benefits.

title: India-based Tanishq jewelry opens its first store in New England
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Tanishq, a leading Indian jewelry brand, opens its first New England store at 303 Turnpike Road, Westborough.
The 4,000-square-foot showroom offers over 5,000 designs catering to bridal, festive, and everyday wear.
This marks Tanishq's 10th U.S. location, expanding the presence of Indian brands amid a growing diaspora.

title: NH athlete is running Boston for the care that keeps her breathing
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Alison Clode, 50, from Portsmouth, NH, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Mass Eye and Ear.
Clode receives care for subglottic stenosis at Mass Eye and Ear, aiding her ability to stay active.
She aims to raise funds for Dr. Song's research on breathing-related conditions through her marathon participation.

title: A major North Shore office park is adding 60 new condos
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Cummings Properties is adding a 60-unit condominium building for 55-plus buyers at Cumming Center in Beverly.
Nearly half of the new condos, priced between $500,000 and $1.5 million, are already claimed.
The development features five levels, offering mostly two-bedroom units and nine subsidized residences.

title: Connelly Early named to Red Sox starting rotation
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Connelly Early, 23, named to the Red Sox starting rotation, set to pitch Sunday against the Reds.
Early, a promising left-hander, struck out 11 in his MLB debut last year with no runs allowed.
Johan Oviedo will move to the bullpen; rotation setup to be reevaluated after several games.

title: Experience an ‘unforgettable’ Easter weekend trip in this Maine city, according to Booking.com
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Portland, Maine, was named a top Easter weekend destination by Booking.com for its charm and scenic coastal views.
Visitors can explore Portland's waterfront, historic areas, and enjoy seafood, lighthouse views, and coastal drives.
Portland hotels and restaurants have received notable recognitions, including Michelin keys and James Beard Foundation semifinalist honors.
